description: Laws governing road safety, traffic violations, motor accident claims, and vehicle insurance in India.

sections.title: Necessity of Driving Licence
sections.text: No person shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place unless he holds an effective driving licence issued to him authorizing him to drive the vehicle.
sections.simplified: You cannot drive any vehicle on a public road without a valid driving licence. Driving without a licence is a punishable offence.
sections.punishment: Fine up to Rs. 5,000 (first offence), Rs. 10,000 (second offence)

sections.title: Dangerous Driving
sections.text: Whoever drives a motor vehicle at a speed or in a manner which is dangerous to the public, having regard to all the circumstances, shall be punishable.
sections.simplified: Rash or dangerous driving is a serious offence. If caught, you face heavy fines and potential imprisonment, especially if it causes injury or death.
sections.punishment: First offence: Rs. 1,000-5,000 and/or 6 months imprisonment. Causing death: up to 7 years and Rs. 10 lakh fine

sections.title: Drunken Driving
sections.text: Whoever, while driving or attempting to drive a motor vehicle, has in his blood alcohol exceeding 30mg per 100ml of blood, as detected in a breath analyser test, shall be punishable.
sections.simplified: Driving drunk (blood alcohol above 30mg/100ml) is a criminal offence. First offence carries jail up to 6 months and/or Rs. 10,000 fine. Repeat offence: up to 2 years jail and/or Rs. 15,000 fine.
sections.punishment: First offence: up to 6 months and/or Rs. 10,000. Repeat: up to 2 years and/or Rs. 15,000

sections.title: Using Vehicle Without Insurance
sections.text: No person shall use or cause or allow any other person to use a motor vehicle in a public place unless there is in force a policy of insurance complying with the requirements of this Chapter.
sections.simplified: Every vehicle on the road MUST have at least third-party insurance. Driving without insurance is a serious offence with heavy fines.
sections.punishment: Fine of Rs. 2,000 and/or imprisonment up to 3 months

sections.title: No-Fault Motor Accident Compensation
sections.text: The owner of the motor vehicle or the authorized insurer shall be liable to pay compensation in respect of death or permanent disablement caused by an accident arising from the use of motor vehicle. Death: Rs. 5 lakh minimum. Permanent disability: Rs. 2.5 lakh minimum.
sections.simplified: If you are injured or a family member dies in a motor accident, you are entitled to compensation regardless of who was at fault. Minimum: Rs. 5 lakh for death, Rs. 2.5 lakh for permanent disability.

sections.title: Application for Compensation (MACT)
sections.text: An application for compensation arising out of an accident may be made by the person who has sustained injury, or by the owner of the property, or in case of death, by all or any of the legal representatives of the deceased. Application to be made to the Claims Tribunal (MACT).
sections.simplified: After a motor accident, you can file a compensation claim with the Motor Accidents Claims Tribunal (MACT). The injured person, property owner, or family members of the deceased can file.

sections.title: General Provision for Traffic Violations
sections.text: Whoever contravenes any provision of this Act or of any rule, regulation or notification made thereunder shall, if no penalty is provided for the offence, be punishable with fine which may extend to Rs. 500 for a first offence and Rs. 1,500 for subsequent offences.
sections.simplified: Any traffic violation that doesn't have a specific penalty has a general fine of Rs. 500 (first time) and Rs. 1,500 (repeat). Specific offences like wrong-side driving, no helmet, red light jumping have higher penalties.

sections.title: Not Wearing Seat Belt
sections.text: Whoever drives a motor vehicle or rides in a motor vehicle without wearing a seat belt shall be punishable with a fine of one thousand rupees.
sections.simplified: Not wearing a seat belt while driving or as a front passenger is punishable with a fine of Rs. 1,000.

sections.title: Not Wearing Helmet
sections.text: Whoever drives or rides on a two-wheeled motor vehicle without a protective headgear conforming to standards shall be punishable with a fine of one thousand rupees and the driving licence may be disqualified for three months.
sections.simplified: Riding a two-wheeler without a proper helmet is punishable with Rs. 1,000 fine and your driving licence can be suspended for 3 months.
sections.punishment: Fine Rs. 1,000; licence suspension up to 3 months

sections.title: Overloading and Overcrowding
sections.text: Whoever drives a vehicle in contravention of speed limits or permits excess passengers shall be punishable. Overloading goods vehicles: fine of Rs. 2,000 plus Rs. 1,000 per extra tonne.
sections.simplified: Carrying excess passengers or overloading a goods vehicle is punishable. For goods vehicles, the fine is Rs. 2,000 base plus Rs. 1,000 for every extra tonne of weight.
